Chapterhouse Staff.name: Chapterhouse Staff
Chapterhouse Staff.description: [Epic] A staff of chapterwood — a dark, dense timber rare beyond the Brotherhood's holds and the Queen's College archives. The grain is almost invisible to sight, visible only to the hand passing over it. Wrought with brass and silver in patterns that catch the light, inlaid with silver wire in sigils that mark the bearer's rank and house. Fewer than a hundred exist outside Telinor and Avenor. To see one carried is to see a master of the order, someone who has shaped doctrine, trained generations, or stood at the high councils. The staff hums faintly, even in stillness — old magic in wood that remembers its crafting.

Ash Staff.name: Ash Staff
Ash Staff.description: [Common] A rough-hewn staff of pale ash, barely worked and fitted with iron bands at neck and foot. Issued to freshly elevated Brothers as their first working staff, or sold to apprentice-mages by smiths and brotherhouses across the continent. The wood is straight-grained and honest, the fittings purely functional. In a wizard's hand it hums with modest power; in any other grasp it is merely a walking stick. This is the tool of training, of first mistakes, of learning the weight and balance that separates a staff-bearer from a fool with a stick.

Journeyman's Staff.name: Journeyman's Staff
Journeyman's Staff.description: [Uncommon] A solid staff of straight-grained oak, properly fitted and sealed with clear lacquer. Quartermasters issue these to Brothers posted beyond Telinor; most active field wizards carry one. The wood has been worked to a subtle taper, the grip wrapped in leather darkened by long use, the iron ferule worn at the point where strikes connect. The staff feels balanced in the hand, neither too light nor too heavy. Witches outside Avenor, Hedge-Wizards, and the Consort line carry equivalent staves, marking them as working mages who have passed their trials.

Master's Staff.name: Master's Staff
Master's Staff.description: [Rare] A staff of heartwood cut from old-growth forests, its grain running true and deep. Hand-fitted by masters of the craft at Telinor or the Queen's College, cored and balanced to exacting standards. The wood is dark — aged to near-black in places — and the brass fittings are intricately worked. Only a senior Brother bearing rank, a full Consort of standing, or a witch of proven mastery carries such a staff. The working flows through it like breath through a living thing. Enemies who know Hedoria's ways see this staff and know they face someone who has fought a decade or more.

Staff of the First Spring.name: Staff of the First Spring
Staff of the First Spring.description: [Legendary] A staff recovered from a pre-Ensoulment vault, its makers dead centuries and their methods lost to the wizard-witch divide. The wood is unknown — it does not splinter, does not age, and does not dull. The fittings are wrought in alloys no current smith can match, inscribed with workings in a script no living scholar reads. Men of knowledge believe it dates from the age when wizardry and the woman's craft flowed from the same spring, before Avenoria's working split them irrevocably. Each such staff carries a name in the tongues of the Before, and each one a story that predates nations. The working flows through it like starlight through water — clear, effortless, and ancient beyond measure. A wizard who bears one has touched the edge of something Hedoria no longer fully understands.

Aphrodisiac Draught.name: Aphrodisiac Draught
Aphrodisiac Draught.description: A pale amber liquid in a glass vial, its scent warm and herbaceous with an undertone of something floral. Mixed from distilled essences of night-blooming plants and fermented honeywine, the draught takes effect within minutes when drunk. It lowers the drinker's resistance to sexual persuasion and advances, leaving them more willing than they might otherwise be. Street-corner witches and apothecaries sell this openly; some use it on themselves before a shift, others on unwilling marks. The effect fades within hours, leaving no lasting harm.

Contraceptive Draught.name: Contraceptive Draught
Contraceptive Draught.description: A bitter herbal infusion, dark brown and faintly metallic-tasting. Hedge-witches and town apothecaries brew this in batches; it prevents conception for several days when drunk. Any woman who works in the trade knows to keep a supply close. The recipe is old and reliable, passed between practitioners and written in every working witch's grimoire. Cost is low; availability across the continent nearly universal.

Soothing Salve.name: Soothing Salve
Soothing Salve.description: A pale cream in a tin, blended from calendula, silk-leaf, and rendered fat. Applied to abraded or bruised skin, it eases soreness and accelerates healing. Used by workers in the carnal trade to mend minor injuries from rough sessions, by healers to treat simple wounds, and by anyone who wants to move without pain the morning after vigorous exertion. Cheap, common, and matter-of-fact. One tin heals incidental damage from a scene and speeds willpower recovery.

Lubricant Oil.name: Lubricant Oil
Lubricant Oil.description: A clear to amber oil with a faint almond scent, blended from seed oils and herb-infusions. Applied before or during physical activity, it reduces friction and damage. Trade workers keep quantities of it on hand; it is as unremarkable in a brothel or carnal-witch's quarters as rope is in a ship. One vial provides protection during heavy sexual sessions or rough use.

Sleeping Draught.name: Sleeping Draught
Sleeping Draught.description: A sweet, pale liquid in a glass bottle, tasting of honey and herbs with a bitter undertone. Slipped into food or drink, it leaves the drinker drowsy within minutes and deeply asleep within the hour. Common in any apothecary or witches' quarter, used by healers to ease pain, by carnal workers to pacify troublesome patrons, and by thieves to rob quietly. The sleep is natural and leaves no lasting damage; the sleeper wakes within a few hours refreshed.

Bedroom Restraints.name: Bedroom Restraints
Bedroom Restraints.description: [Common] Soft leather cuffs and binding straps, finely tooled and fitted with brass buckles and D-rings. Stitched with reinforced thread and designed for repeated use without fraying. Carnal practitioners carry these as working tools to pin and hold a partner during pleasure or training. They restrain without bruising if fitted carefully. Used in conjunction with the Suppress and Pin and Ride abilities, they grant tactical advantage in capture-play scenarios.

Witch's Kiss-Mark.name: Witch's Kiss-Mark
Witch's Kiss-Mark.description: A small wooden palette of enchanted lip-paint, ash-grey and shimmering faintly in candlelight. Applied to the lips, it leaves a mark on anyone kissed by the wearer. The mark binds the kissed person into a light enchantment of charm lasting one scene, lowering their willpower defense against the wearer's persuasion and enticements. Each use consumes the paint entirely; a fresh palette must be crafted. Sold quietly in carnal-witch quarters and black-market apothecaries, expensive enough to reserve for high-value targets or special nights.

Quell-Perfume.name: Quell-Perfume
Quell-Perfume.description: A vial of dark, thick liquid with a cloying floral scent that clings to skin and clothes. Distilled from Quelled-creature secretions, it is forbidden in most kingdoms and freely bought only in places where the Quelled are trafficked openly. Applied to the skin, the wearer becomes powerfully attractive to anyone they embrace, who risks becoming aphrodised without drinking or being dosed. Repeated use is addictive, degrading the wearer's willpower each time. Possession carries criminal penalties in any land with enforcement authority.

Iron Ring of the Brotherhood.name: Iron Ring of the Brotherhood
Iron Ring of the Brotherhood.description: [Rare] A heavy iron signet ring, its face stamped with the Brotherhood's glyph in mirror-script that identifies rank and standing. Issued to full Brothers and close allies of the order, the ring carries a subtle working that shields the wearer from Carnal Arts specifically wielded by witches. Non-witch practitioners can still affect the wearer; only witch-channeled effects are blocked. The ring also advertises the wearer's allegiance openly. In some kingdoms this marks them as a man of power; in others, as an enemy to be monitored.

Joy-Wood Phallus.name: Joy-Wood Phallus
Joy-Wood Phallus.description: [Rare] A carved implement of joy-wood, polished smooth and inscribed with minor workings that hum faintly to the touch. Joy-wood is wood harvested from the same rare forests that yield ingredients for witch-craft; it carries residual enchantment that amplifies carnal effects. Use of this device allows the Sister's Draw ability at lower thresholds of carnal-arts and witchcraft rank. Possession raises eyebrows in any settled place; in lands with College authority, it is quietly restricted and subject to confiscation on search.
